title: Poongatru Un Per Solla Lyrics Vetri Vizha
stanzas: <div>Poongatru Un Per Solla Lyrics Vetri Vizha

Poongaatru un per solla
kaetteney indru.
neerootru en thol konja
paartheney indru.

moongil kaattrom
maynaakkal rendu
moga panpaaduthe.
melai kaattrodu
kaiserthu naanal
kaadhal kondaaduthe.

yedhedho enna thondra
yegaantham ingae.
naan kaanum vannam
yaavum neethaane anbae.

jenma jenmangal aanaalum
enna jeevan unnoduthaan.
devai sridevi poovaaram sooda
dhevan ennoduthaan.

neela vaan kooda
niram maari pogum.
nesam niramaarumaa
kaala kaalangal
ponaalum enna
kadhal thadam maarumaa.

oiyaamal unnai konjum
oodhaapu vannam.
rajavin muththam kollum
roja poo kannam.

Poongaatru un per solla
kaetteney indru.
neerootru en thol konja
paartheney indru.

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song expresses deep romantic feelings and the beauty of love, capturing moments of longing and affection.

In which movie is this song featured?

This song is featured in the movie Vetri Vizha.

Who are the singers of this song?

The song is sung by the legendary singers K.S. Chithra and S.P. Balasubrahmanyam.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for this song was composed by the renowned Ilaiyaraaja.

When was this song released?

This song was released on October 28, 1989.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Poongatru Un Per Solla, the characters express their deep affection and yearning for each other, making it a quintessential romantic ballad. The soothing melody, crafted by Ilaiyaraaja, enhances the emotional depth of the lyrics, creating a captivating listening experience. This song beautifully encapsulates the themes of love and nostalgia, evoking a sense of longing that resonates with listeners. Its timeless appeal makes it a perfect choice for weddings and romantic occasions.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Poongatru Un Per Solla is a timeless melody that captures the essence of romance and nostalgia. With the enchanting voices of K.S. Chithra and S.P. Balasubrahmanyam, this song from the classic film Vetri Vizha resonates with heartfelt emotions. Experience the magic of Ilaiyaraaja's composition that beautifully intertwines love and longing.
stanzaMeanings: Today, I am asking your name, my dear. I looked at my shoulder a little today.
In the temple, there is honey in the offerings, that is the joy of marriage.
I looked at my shoulder a little today.
In the bamboo grove, two birds are singing. The one above is singing about love.
Is this the time when the parrot group sings with joy?
Is this the time when the wings flutter and sing?
Something is happening here. The colors I see are all you, my love.
Without changing colors, desires will keep increasing again and again.
Today, I am asking your name, my dear. I looked at my shoulder a little today.
In the cycle of births, my life is with you. Goddess Sridevi, the flower, is with me.
The blue sky will change colors. Will love change even if time passes?
Without fading, your color shines. The king's kiss is like a rose petal.
The garden of bananas that cannot be cut. It shows all the joys.
Today, I am asking your name, my dear. I looked at my shoulder a little today.
In the temple, there is honey in the offerings, that is the joy of marriage.
Today, I am asking your name, my dear.
